title: Meathead: The Science of Great Barbecue and Grilling
author: Meathead Goldwyn (with Prof. Greg Blonder)
note: Catalog built from the table of contents (titles + page numbers only). To add any of these as a real cookwhat recipe, scan the full recipe page(s) so the actual ingredient list + method can be captured. Now covers Chicken & Turkey (ch.10), Seafood (ch.11), and Sides (ch.12). COMPLETE: full TOC captured through the end of the Sides chapter (book ends with Index, p.367).
source_format: Source for any meal cataloged from this book: "Meathead (Goldwyn) — p.NNN" (no URL).

recipes.title: Big Bad Beef Rub
recipes.fitsMenu: saved for a future brisket/ribs cook (pulled off the ribeye - too bold for a quick steak)

ingredients: 2 tsp chili powder or ground ancho
steps: Mix all the ingredients together in a bowl. (No salt - salt the meat separately.)
tips: Built for brisket and beef ribs - it's more potent than a Dalmatian (salt-pepper) rub and forms a rich, crunchy bark.
Beef doesn't love sweetness like pork: the little sugar is only there to help bark formation, not to sweeten.
Ancho gives a raisiny note; chipotle adds smoky heat (better flavor than cayenne).

recipes.title: Simon & Garfunkel Rub
recipes.fitsMenu: herb rub for poultry; used by Grilled Asparagus & Cornell Chicken

steps: Pulse everything in a blender or food processor for a few seconds until you have a coarse powder.
tips: Parsley, sage, rosemary & thyme - an all-purpose herb rub for pork, turkey, and chicken.
Also great on sauteed veggies, scrambled eggs, grilled potatoes, and grilled asparagus.
Keep it a coarse powder so it adheres and you can see the herbs.

recipes.title: Hawaiian Huli-Huli Teriyaki Sauce and Marinade
Roasted Red Pepper and Garlic Coulis

steps: Combine all the ingredients in a jar with a lid and shake thoroughly to emulsify.
Shake again just before using, then drizzle over grilled seafood.
tips: Classic Greek 2:1 oil-to-lemon sauce - shake hard so it emulsifies.
If you use dried oregano, halve the quantity.
Excellent on simple grilled fish, shrimp, lobster, or calamari; also good with parsley, basil, or fennel.

recipes.title: Pork Tenderloin with Cowboy Java Rub
Big, Thick Steakhouse Steaks
recipes.fitsMenu: the technique for the July 4 ribeye

recipes.title: The Afterburner Method for Skinny Steaks
Santa Maria Tri-Tip (Poor Man's Prime Rib)

steps: Soften the butter at room temp ~20 min (or microwave in 5-second blasts) until spreadable but not melted.
Mash in the herbs with a fork until evenly combined.
Scoop onto a 12-inch sheet of plastic wrap and roll into a ~1-inch log (like a Tootsie Roll); twist the ends.
Refrigerate overnight. Keeps in the fridge or freezer.
tips: It's a template - swap in chipotle, ancho, dried mushroom, port, miso, or cheese for variations.
Slice a coin onto hot-off-the-grill steaks so it melts over the top.
Also great on grilled vegetables, potatoes, or even waffles.

recipes.title: Simon & Garfunkel Chicken
recipes.fitsMenu: alt for the Fri grilled chicken

steps: Whisk the egg, oil, vinegar, salt, S&G Rub, and pepper into an emulsified marinade (reserve some for basting).
Marinate the chicken quarters 3-24 hours.
Set up the grill for two-zone cooking, ~325F on the indirect side.
Grill the chicken on the indirect side, flipping and basting with the reserved marinade, until breasts hit 160F and thighs 165F.
Rest briefly and serve.
tips: The egg emulsifies the oil-and-vinegar marinade so it clings to and bastes the bird (Bob Baker's Cornell trick).
Cook two-zone indirect (~325F) so the acidic, eggy marinade doesn't scorch.
Reserve marinade for basting before it touches raw chicken - don't reuse the dipping marinade.

recipes.title: Sweet Georgia Brown Smoked Yard Bird
Big Bob Gibson's Chicken in 'Bama White Sauce
Grilled Duck Breasts in Cherry-Port Sauce
Chef Bonner's Fish Fillets with Brioni Broth

ingredients: 1 lb skinless mild white fish (snapper, mahi-mahi, grouper, or tilapia)
1 tbsp mayonnaise (plus more to coat the fish)
2 1/2 tsp finely chopped chipotle in adobo
pico de gallo or red salsa
steps: Season the fish with salt, then dust with the ancho and black pepper.
Stir together the sour cream, mayo, and chipotle; season with salt and refrigerate.
Set up a two-zone grill with a metal grill topper over the hot (direct) side.
Coat the fish in a thin layer of mayo and grill on the topper until just cooked (internal ~125-130F).
Warm the tortillas; halve, slice, and lightly dress the avocado with lime.
Assemble: fish, chipotle crema, shredded cabbage, avocado, and pico; serve with lime wedges.
tips: A thin mayo coat helps the fish brown and keeps it from sticking to the grate.
Use a metal grill topper so the delicate fillets don't fall through or fall apart.
Pull the fish at ~125-130F so it stays moist, and warm the tortillas so they're pliable.

recipes.title: Grilled Oysters with White Wine Sauce
Championship Bacon-Wrapped Stuffed Shrimp
recipes.flag: cream cheese (dairy); it's an appetizer, not a dinner main

ingredients: 20 shell-on large (16/20 count) shrimp, preferably wild Gulf
finely chopped chives or flat-leaf parsley (optional)
steps: Peel and devein the shrimp, leaving the tails on; brine (p.343).
Halve and seed the jalapenos; cut into strips.
Butterfly each shrimp, fill with cream cheese and a jalapeno strip, wrap in a slice of bacon, and secure with a toothpick.
Set up the grill for two-zone cooking, ~325F on the indirect side.
Cook on the indirect side until the bacon is rendered and crisp and the shrimp are just cooked.
Brush with Thai sweet chili sauce and garnish with chives or parsley.
tips: Cook indirect so the bacon fully renders before the shrimp overcook.
Brine the shrimp (p.343) so they stay plump and seasoned; secure the bacon firmly with toothpicks.
Pepper-crusted bacon + a sweet-chili glaze is the award-winning combo.

recipes.title: Grilled Asparagus
recipes.fitsMenu: the green side for the July 4 ribeye

ingredients: 1 tbsp balsamic condimento or balsamic reduction
steps: If using inexpensive salad-grade balsamic, reduce it to a syrup (good condimento/tradizionale can be used straight).
Snap off the woody bottoms (1-3 in). Coat the spears with oil, sprinkle with the rub and salt, and shave the cheese into wide ribbons; set the cheese aside.
Fire up for two-zone cooking, ~325F on the indirect side, and grill the asparagus.
Serve topped with the Parmesan ribbons and a drizzle of the balsamic.
tips: Pick fat spears of even thickness - they grill more uniformly and stay less stringy than skinny ones.
Reduce cheap balsamic to a syrup; quality condimento/tradizionale can go on straight.
Shave the Parm into wide ribbons (peeler or box grater) for melt and presentation.
